rent.note: furnished 1BR in District 1/2 or Thao Dien; avg $420; D1 premium ~$500-600

utilities.note: electric + water + fiber internet; hot city, A/C runs hard

diningOut.note: GrabBike $0.50-1.50 rides; pho $1-2; banh mi $0.50; mix of local + cafe

transit.note: Grab bike/car dominates; 5km ride $1.80; monthly budget

coworking.note: robust coworking scene; Toong, Base, The Hive all popular; ~$100-180/mo

misc.note: gym, rooftop bars, coworking events, personal care, weekend trips, buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers applied to RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Raise for peak tourist/weather months, lower for off-peak.

evidence.claim: HCMC average 1BR apartment $420/month; District 1/Thao Dien 20-40% more
evidence.quote: The average 1-bedroom apartment costs $420.00/month, which is below the global average. Western-style districts (District 1, District 2, Thao Dien) cost 20–40% more
Digital nomads should budget $1,200-1,400 USD per month, while local expats can live on $850-950 USD
Motorbike rides (GrabBike) cost $0.50–$1.50 for short trips, and a 5km Uber ride costs $1.80

evidence.claim: HCMC comfortable single expat $900-1,400/month depending on neighborhood
evidence.quote: A single expat can live in Ho Chi Minh City very comfortably on $900–$1,400 per month, depending on neighborhood and lifestyle
